stimulus: The Pearl Saloon was one of the most prominent African American–owned businesses in late nineteenth-century San Francisco. ______blank by businesswoman Marion Whitfield, the saloon was praised for its refined service and welcoming atmosphere.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The past participle “established” correctly forms a supplementary modifier describing the saloon (the saloon was established by Whitfield).

Choice A (present singular) and Choice D (bare plural) are finite verb forms that would require a subject. Choice C (present participle “establishing”) would incorrectly imply the saloon was performing the establishing.
